profile.name: James
profile.role_in_household: Primary client; manages most of the household’s financial logistics and feels responsible for planning around ongoing alimony obligations.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate-to-high: comfortable discussing account types (taxable vs. retirement) and cash flow, but may gloss over tax nuances and paperwork details unless prompted.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate risk taker in principle, but becomes noticeably more conservative when the conversation turns to alimony payments and near-term liquidity; prefers a buffer and dislikes feeling 'locked in.'
profile.communication_style: Direct and numbers-forward; will cite specific figures and correct small inaccuracies. Uses occasional hedging like 'roughly' or 'give or take' when discussing timing or administrative details; can get terse when discussing the divorce/alimony topic.
emotional_tendencies: relief when there’s a clear plan and cash cushion
profile.decision_making_style: Analytical and comparison-based; wants to see tradeoffs and a simple decision framework, then moves quickly once he trusts the recommendation.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Cautiously open: willing to share details but expects competence and discretion, especially around sensitive family obligations.
hidden_concerns: worries about being overextended if rental income fluctuates
concerned about how alimony affects long-term retirement timing
uneasy about discussing the private markets/alternatives holding if it can’t be accessed quickly

profile.name: Linda
profile.role_in_household: Spouse/partner; steady earner and day-to-day budget anchor, focused on stability and making sure plans feel realistic month to month.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate: understands budgeting and basic investing concepts, but may mix up terms (gross vs. net, balance vs. contribution) and prefers plain-language explanations.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate but more safety-leaning than James; tends to favor predictable outcomes and may push for simpler allocations and clearer emergency reserves before growth moves.
profile.communication_style: Warm, collaborative, and story-based; speaks in round numbers and approximations ('around,' 'about,' 'something like'). Asks clarifying questions and occasionally interrupts to confirm she understood; may defer to James on exact figures.
emotional_tendencies: anxiety when plans feel too complex
reassurance-seeking through checklists and next steps
profile.decision_making_style: Consensus-driven; wants to align as a couple and will look for advisor guidance on priorities and sequencing rather than optimizing every detail.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Generally trusting once rapport is established; values transparency and dislikes jargon or feeling talked down to.
internal_biases: status quo bias (prefers not to change too many things at once)
hidden_concerns: worries that the alimony obligation will crowd out their shared goals
concerned about having enough liquid cash for surprises despite having investments
quietly concerned about being financially dependent on James’s decisions even though she contributes income
